channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Controller of Budget flags risk of government debt default over Sh3 trillion
item.description: Kenya is staring at a serious debt problem, with the government potentially defaulting on Sh3.32 trillion of external debt due within the next year, warns Controller of Budget Margaret Nyakang’o. She ...

item.title: Radio doctor charged with 102 counts for failure to file tax and VAT returns
item.description: Dr Leonard Mandlalele Mhinga, aged 73 and known for his radio broadcasts, has been formally charged with 102 offences for failing to submit both income tax and value-added tax (VAT) returns over a pro...

item.title: Michelle Tsiakkas departs from Strictly Come Dancing ahead of 2026 series
item.description: Strictly Come Dancing has confirmed that professional dancer Michelle Tsiakkas will not be returning for the 2026 series following a cast overhaul. Alongside previous departures of Karen Hauer, Nadiya...

item.title: Japan Deploys Long-Range Missiles to Strengthen National Defence
item.description: Japan has deployed upgraded Type-12 land-to-ship missiles at Camp Kengun in Kumamoto prefecture, marking a significant enhancement of its offensive capabilities. These missiles, developed by Mitsubish...

item.title: Senator Imee Marcos expresses regret to VP Sara Duterte over 2022 election involvement and political fallout
item.description: Senator Imee Marcos publicly apologized to Vice President Sara Duterte for persuading her to run as vice presidential candidate in the 2022 national elections under the Uniteam alliance with President...

item.title: Botswana’s Economy Contracts Due to Mining Sector Downturn
item.description: Botswana’s economy faced a notable contraction in the final quarter of 2025, with real GDP declining by 5.4% year-on-year. The main culprit behind this downturn was the mining and quarrying sector, wh...

item.title: El-Rufai Claims Security Operatives Planned to Arrest His Brother During Funeral
item.description: Former Kaduna State governor, Nasir El-Rufai, don talk say security people dey plan to arrest him elder brother, Bashir El-Rufai, to force negotiation with am. El-Rufai make this clear for one viral v...

item.title: FBI classifies Michigan synagogue vehicle-ramming as Hezbollah-inspired terrorist attack
item.description: On March 12, 2026, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a 41-year-old Lebanese-born U.S. citizen, drove a pickup truck loaded with fireworks and gasoline into Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Mi...

item.title: Western Cape Taxi Operators Maintain Fares Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Taxi operators in the Western Cape have decided not to hike fares despite a significant increase in fuel prices, aiming to ease financial pressure on commuters. Petrol prices are expected to rise by a...

item.title: India supplies 7,000 tonnes of diesel to Bangladesh via pipeline amid West Asia fuel disruptions
item.description: Bangladesh has begun receiving a fresh supply of 7,000 tonnes of diesel from India through the India-Bangladesh Friendship Pipeline, originating at the Numaligarh refinery in Assam. The consignment st...

item.title: IEBC Sees Strong Start as Over 300 Kenyans Register in Kirinyaga
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) in Kirinyaga County kicked off its month-long mass voter registration drive with a promising start, registering over 300 new voters on the ve...

item.title: Fact Check: Trump Did Not Make Any Statements About Kenya's Politics
item.description: Some graphics have been circulating on social media here in Kenya claiming that former US President Donald Trump hinted at getting involved in our political affairs and asked Kenyans to send him updat...

item.title: Iran–US–Israel war escalates with tanker attack off Dubai, missile exchanges and soaring global energy prices
item.description: The article provides rolling coverage of the rapidly escalating war involving the US, Israel and Iran, and its spillover across the wider Middle East region. It reports that Iran attacked and set abla...

item.title: Public Schools Struggle with Chronic Underfunding as Term One Ends
item.description: As public schools in Kenya close for the April holidays, the same perennial financial challenges continue to plague them. Many boarding schools have sent learners home early due to insufficient funds ...

item.title: Sudbury's Nickel City Cats rescue reaches 93 intakes in March, including 50 from one home
item.description: A non-profit animal rescue organization based in Sudbury, northern Ontario, known as Nickel City Cats, has taken in an extraordinary 93 cats during the month of March 2026 alone. The group, which oper...

item.title: China outlaws use of apartments for storing cremated remains
item.description: Chinese authorities have officially banned the practice of entombing cremated human remains in unoccupied residential flats, a trend that had become popular as families looked for cheaper alternatives...

item.title: Naheed Nenshi says Avi Lewis’s stance on energy doesn’t align with Alberta’s priorities
item.description: Former Calgary mayor and Liberal Member of Parliament Naheed Nenshi has criticized newly elected Alberta NDP leader Avi Lewis, arguing that his views on energy policy could harm the province’s economi...

item.title: Eurovision expands to Asia with inaugural contest in Bangkok featuring 10 nations
item.description: Eurovision is making its debut in Asia with the launch of the first-ever Asian edition of the iconic song contest, scheduled to take place in Bangkok later in 2026. The Eurovision Song Contest Asia 20...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Positions Herself as Key Leader Across West Bengal Assembly Seats
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, during a rally in Chandrakona, Paschim Medinipur, urged voters to support her leadership across all 294 assembly constituencies, emphasising leadership over...

item.title: Authorities Confirm Mnarani Bridge in Kilifi is Safe, Online Image is Fake
item.description: Kenyans have been warned not to believe claims circulating on social media about a large crack on Mnarani bridge in Kilifi County. Posts on platforms like X and Facebook have been showing an image tha...
